subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Assertion A: The consumption of water increases with increase in the distribution pressure.
Reason R: Higher distribution pressure causes more loss and waste of water.
Select your answer according to the coding system given below

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is true.

solution: As the pressure increases in distribution system more loss and waste of water occurs which results in increases the consumption of water

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
The per capita consumption of a locality is affected by
i) Climatic conditions
ii) Quality of water
iii) Distribution pressure
The correct answer is
solution:
The per capita consumption of a locality is affected by:
- Size of the city
- Climatic conditions
- Types of gentry and habits of people (population)
- Industrial and commercial activities
- Quality of water supplies
- Pressure in the distribution system
- Development of sewerage facilities
- System of supply
- Cost of water
- Policy of metering and method of charging

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following causes a decrease in Per capita consumption?

options.c: Better standard of living of the people

solution: Metering system of water supply causes a decrease in Per capita consumption.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The hourly variation factor is usually taken is
solution:
The hourly variation factor is usually taken is 1.5.
- The daily variation factor is usually taken as 1.8
- The monthly variation factor is usually taken as 1.4

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If the average daily consumption of a city is 100,000 $m^3$, the maximum daily consumption on peak hourly demand will be
solution: The maximum daily consumption on peak hourly demand will be = $2.7 \times \text{Average daily demand} = 2.7 \times 100,000 = 270000\text{ }m^3$

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The distribution mains are designed for

options.d: Maximum hourly demand on maximum day

solution: The distribution mains should be designed for maximum hourly draft of maximum day or coincident draft with fire whichever is more.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: As compared to geometrical increase method of forecasting population, arithmetical increase method gives
solution: As compared to geometrical increase method of forecasting population, arithmetical increase method gives lesser value.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The population of a town in three consecutive years are 5000, 7000 and 8400 respectively. The population of the town in the fourth consecutive year according to geometrical increase method is
solution:
Arithmetic Mean = $(0.4 + 0.2) / 2 = 0.3$
Population at fourth consecutive year = $8400 \times (1 + 0.3) = 10920$
Or by geometric mean:
Geometrical mean = $\sqrt{0.4 \times 0.2} = 0.2828 = 28.28\%$
Population in fourth year is = $8400 \times (1 + 0.2828) = 10775.878$
So, the option d should be best choice

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The suitable method of forecasting population for a young and rapidly increasing city is
solution:
Arithmetic method = for well-developed city
Geometrical method = for a young and rapidly increasing city
Incremental Increase method = used when type of city not known

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The depression of water table in a well due to pumping will be maximum

options.a: At a distance R from the well
options.c: At a distance R/2 from the well

solution: When the well is pumped the water level in the well is lowered, by lowering this water level a gradient occurs between the water in the surrounding aquifer and the water in the well because water flows from high head to low head, this gradient produces a flow from the surrounding aquifer into the well as the water flow into the well the water level in aquifer around the well is decreased.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The devices which are installed for drawing water from the sources are called
solution: Intakes are the devices which are installed for drawing water from the sources.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Select the correct relationship between porosity (N), specific yield (y) and specific retention (R)
solution:
Relationship between porosity (N), specific yield (y) and specific retention (R)
Porosity = Specific yield + Specific retention
i.e N = y + R

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The type of valve, which is provided on the suction pipe in a tube-well, is
solution:
Reflux valve/Check valve/Non-Returning valve
- Used for unidirectional flow.
- Provided on the suction pipe in a tube well.
- Automatic device.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The maximum discharge of a tube-well is about
solution: The maximum discharge of a tube-well is about 50 litres/sec.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: As compared to shallow-wells, deep wells have
solution: As compared to shallow-wells, deep wells have more discharge.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Ground water is usually free from
solution: Ground water is usually free from suspended impurities due to natural filtration property of soil.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The polluted water is one which

options.b: Consists of undesirable substances rendering it unfit for drinking and domestic use
options.c: Is safe and suitable for drinking and domestic use

solution: The polluted water is one which consists of undesirable substances rendering it unfit for drinking and domestic use.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following is not a water borne disease?
solution:
Dysentery, Cholera and Typhoid are water borne disease.
Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites. The parasites are spread to people through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The most common cause of acidity in water is
solution: The most common cause of acidity in water is carbon dioxide present in atmosphere.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The phenolic compounds in public water supply should not be more than
solution: The phenolic compounds in public water supply should not be more than 0.001 ppm

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The maximum permissible limit for flouride in drinking water is
solution:
Fluorides content exceeding 1.5 mg/l - cause mottling of teeth and fluorosis.
- Fluorides content lower concentrations lesser than 1 mg/l - cause dental cavities (tooth decay due to cavity formation).

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Standard EDTA (ethylene diamine tetra acetic acid) solution is used to determine the
solution:
Hardness of water is determined by standard EDTA solution
- Indicator used in determination of hardness is EBT (Eriochrome black T)

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
If the coliform bacteria is present in a sample of water, then the coliform test to be conducted is
(i) Presumptive coliform test
(ii) Continued coliform test
(iii) Completed coliform test
The correct answer is
solution:
If the coliform bacteria is present in a sample of water, then the coliform test to be conducted is
- Presumptive coliform test
- Continued coliform test
- Completed coliform test

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Alkalinity in water is expressed as milligrams per litre in terms of equivalent of
solution: Alkalinity in water is expressed as milligrams per litre in terms of equivalent of CaCO3

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The product of $H^+$ ions and $OH^-$ ions in a stronger alkali is
solution: In every situation the product of $H^+$ ions and $OH^-$ ions is always $10^{-14}$ at $25^\circ\text{C}$ temperature.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following values of pH represents a stronger acid?
solution:
PH value = 7 neutral
PH < 7 = acidic nature
PH > 7 = alkaline nature

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Turbidity is measured on
solution: Turbidity is measured on Standard silica scale.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: On standard silica scale, the turbidity in drinking water should be limited to

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Residual chlorine in water is determined by
solution:
Methods to determine residual chlorine are-
- Orthotolidine test
- Arsenide Orthotolidine test
- DPD test
- Starch iodide test

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Orthotolidine test is used for determination of
solution: Orthotolidine test is used for determination of residual chlorine.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If the total hardness of water is greater than its total alkalinity, the carbonate hardness will be equal to
solution:
Carbonate hardness = Minimum of both (Total hardness, Total alkalinity)
Non-Carbonate hardness = Total hardness - Total alkalinity
So if Total hardness > Total alkalinity than Carbonate hardness = Total alkalinity

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The amount of residual chlorine left in public water supply for safety against pathogenic bacteria is about
solution:
For Pathogenic bacteria the range varies between 0.2 to 1 mg/l.
For Protection against viral infection minimum free residual chlorine is 0.5 mg/l.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The dissolved oxygen level in natural unpolluted waters at normal temperature is found to be of the order of
solution: The dissolved oxygen level in natural unpolluted waters at normal temperature is 10 mg/litre.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The velocity of flow of water in a sedimentation tank is about
solution: The velocity of flow of water in a sedimentation tank is about 15 to 30 cm/minute.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The length of rectangular sedimentation tank should not be more than
solution: The length of rectangular sedimentation tank should not be more than 4B. Where B is the width of the tank.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The overflow rate for plain sedimentation tanks is about
solution:
Overflow rate for plain sedimentation = 12000 - 18000 lit/day/$m^2$ or 500 - 750 litres/hour/$m^2$.
- Overflow rate for sedimentation with coagulation = 24000 - 30000 lit/day/$m^2$ or 1000 - 1250 litres/hour/$m^2$

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Percentage of bacterial load that can be removed from water by the process of plain sedimentation is about
solution: It is estimated that plain sedimentation tank can remove about 60% of suspended matter and about 75% of bacterial load from water.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The settling velocity of a particle in a sedimentation tank depends on

options.c: Both depth and surface area of tank

solution:
The settling velocity of a particle in a sedimentation tank depends on surface area of tank.
- Sedimentation is a physical water treatment process in which gravity is used to remove suspended solids from water.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The settling velocity of a particle in a sedimentation tank increases if

options.b: The surface area of tank is increased
options.c: The depth of tank is decreased

solution:
As per stokes law $V_s = \frac{g(\rho_p - \rho)D_p^2}{18\mu}$. So denser and larger particles have high settling velocity.
- Settling velocity = $Q / (B \times L)$ ($B \times L$ = Surface area)
- Settling velocity is independent of depth

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: For a given discharge, the efficiency of sedimentation tank can be increased by

options.c: Increasing the surface area of tank
options.d: Decreasing the surface area of tank

solution:
Efficiency = $\frac{\text{Settling velocity of particle}}{\text{Surface overflow rate}}$
Surface overflow rate = $\frac{\text{Flow rate}}{\text{Surface area of tank}}$
So Efficiency $\propto$ Surface area of tank

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
The amount of coagulant needed for coagulation of water increases with
(i) Increase in turbidity of water
(ii) Decrease in turbidity of water
(iii) Increase in temperature of water
(iv) Decrease in temperature of water
The correct answer is
solution:
As the turbidity of water Increases the amount of coagulant is also increases.
Decrease in temperature of water affects viscosity of water which results in increase of coagulant dose.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The detention period and overflow rate respectively for plain sedimentation as compared to sedimentation with coagulation are generally
solution:
Plain sedimentation: Detention period = 4-8 hours, Overflow rate = 500-750 litres/hour/$m^2$
Sedimentation with coagulation: Detention period = 2-4 hours, Overflow rate = 1000-1250 litres/hour/$m^2$

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Alum as a coagulant is found to be an effective when pH range of water is
solution: Alum is effective when pH of water is between 6.5-8.5. Its dose depends on turbidity, color, taste, pH and temperature of water.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The detention period in coagulation tanks is usually kept as
solution:
Detention period:
- for plain sedimentation = 4 to 8 hour
- for sedimentation with coagulant = 3 to 4 hour

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The alum, when added as a coagulant in water

options.a: Does not require alkalinity in water for flocculation
options.b: Does not affect pH value of water

solution:
When alum added as coagulant:
- It decreases ph of water
- It causes permanent hardness

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The chemical most commonly used to increase speed of sedimentation of sewage is
solution: The chemical most commonly used to increase speed of sedimentation of sewage is lime.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In water treatment, rapid gravity filters are adopted to remove

options.c: Floating solids and dissolved inorganic solids

solution: In water treatment, rapid gravity filters are adopted to remove bacteria and colloidal solids.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The rate of filtration in slow sand filters in million litres per day per hectare is about
solution: The rate of filtration in slow sand filters in million litres per day per hectare is about 50 - 60.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The effective size of sand particles used in slow sand filters is
solution: Sand used in slow sand filters should have an effective size of 0.15 - 0.35 mm and a uniformity coefficient of 1.5 - 3, however < 2 is desirable.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
As compared to rapid sand filters, slow sand filters give
i) Slower filtration rate
ii) Higher filtration rate
iii) Lesser efficiency in removal of bacteria
iv) Higher efficiency in removal of bacteria
The correct answer is
solution: Slow sand filter has a slower filtration rate (100 - 200 LPH/$m^2$) compared to rapid sand filter (3000 - 6000 LPH/$m^2$), but has higher efficiency in bacteria removal (98 - 99%) compared to rapid sand filter (80 - 90%).

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Assertion A: Slow sand filters are more efficient in removal of bacteria than rapid sand filters.
Reason R: The sand used in slow sand filters is finer than that in rapid sand filters.
Select your answer based on the coding system given below:

options.a: Both A and R true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is true.

solution:
Bacteria removal efficiency:
Slow sand filter - 98 to 99%
Rapid sand filter - 80 to 90%
Effective size (mm):
Slow sand filter - 0.25 to 0.35
Rapid sand filter - 0.35 to 0.6

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Air binding phenomena in rapid sand filters occur due to
solution: Air binding phenomena in rapid sand filters occur due to More Negative head (>1.2m)

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The percentage of filtered water, which is used for Washing in rapid sand filters, is about
solution: The filtered water used for Washing in rapid sand filters, is about 2 to 4%

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Period of cleaning of slow sand filters is about
solution:
Period of cleaning for:
Slow sand filters: 2 to 3 months
Rapid sand filter: 24 to 48 hours

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The rate of filtration of pressure filters is

options.a: Less than that of slow sand filters
options.b: In between the filtration rate of slow sand filters and rapid sand filters
options.c: Greater than that of rapid sand filters
options.d: Equal to that of slow sand filters

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Double filtration is used

options.a: To increase the filtration capacity of slow sand filters
options.b: To increase the filtration capacity of rapid sand filters
options.c: For isolated buildings like swimming pools, hotels etc.

solution: Sol(a). The water is allowed to pass through two or more slow sand filters arranged one after the other thus increasing filtration capacity of slow sand filter.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Cleaning is done by
(i) Scraping and removal in slow sand filters
(ii) Back washing in slow sand filters
(iii) Scraping and removal in rapid sand filters
(iv) Back washing in rapid sand filters

The correct answer is
solution:
Sol(c). In slow sand filter cleaning is done by scraping and removal of the top 2-3 cm of sand, which is washed and returned to the filter.
In rapid sand filter cleaning is done by backwashing. backwashing consists of reversing the flow of water through the filter causing water to travel from the bottom of the filter to the top.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Disinfection of water results in
solution:
Sol (c). Disinfection effectively kills a large variety of microbial waterborne pathogens.
Chlorine is widely credited with virtually eliminating outbreaks of water borne disease in the United States and other developed countries.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
The disinfection efficiency of chlorine increases by
(i) Decreasing the time of contact
(ii) Decreasing the temperature of water
(iii) Increasing the temperature of water.

The correct answer is
solution:
Sol(d). The disinfection efficiency of chlorine increases by Increasing the temperature of water.
The cause. In general, chemical processes are slowed down due to a decrease in temperature. As a rule of thumb, the reaction rate halves each $10^\circ\text{C}$ of temperature decrease. Yet as shown in the table with chemical disinfectants there is a great difference in behaviour upon temperature decrease.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Chlorine demand of water is equal to

options.c: Sum of applied and residual chlorine
options.d: Difference of applied and residual chlorine

solution:
Sol(d). Chlorine demand is the difference between the amount of chlorine added to water or wastewater and the amount of residual chlorine remaining after a given contact time.
Chlorine demand may change with dosage, time, temperature, pH, and nature and amount of the impurities in the water.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The process in which the chlorination is done beyond the break point is known as
solution:
Sol(c). Process in which the chlorination is done beyond the break point is known as Super chlorination.
Super chlorination, also known as hyperchlorination, temporarily increases the free chlorine residual in a water distribution system. A high free chlorine residual (i.e. above 5 mg/L) is effective against most bacteria and can be used in hot, warm and cold-water distribution systems,

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The percentage of chlorine in fresh bleaching powder is about
solution: Sol(c). Chlorine in fresh bleaching powder is about 30 to 35%

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The treatment of water with bleaching powder is known as
solution: Sol(d). The treatment of water with bleaching powder is known as Hypo chlorination

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The suitable method for disinfection of swimming pool water is
solution: Sol(a). The suitable method for disinfection of swimming pool water is Ultra violet ray's treatment.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following chemical compounds can be used for dichlorination of water?
solution:
Sol(c). Dichlorination is the process of removing residual chlorine from disinfected wastewater prior to discharge into the environment.
Sulfur dioxide is most commonly used for dichlorination and is the major focus of this fact sheet.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In chlorination, with the rise in temperature of water, death rate of bacteria
solution:
Sol(a). As we know that
Disinfection efficiency $\propto$ Temperature
That means as temperature increases death rate is also increases

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: As compared to higher pH values, the contact period required for efficient chlorination at lower pH values is
solution: Sol(a). Contact period required for efficient chlorination at lower pH values is smaller as compared to higher pH values.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Disinfection efficiency is

options.a: Reduced at higher pH value of water
options.b: Unaffected by pH value of water
options.c: Increased at higher pH value of water
options.d: Highest at pH value equal to 7

solution: Sol(a). Disinfection efficiency is Reduced at higher pH value of water because HOCL ion which is more effective in killing pathogenic bacteria is not effective at higher ph value as much as at lower ph value.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In lime-soda process

options.c: Lime reduces the carbonate hardness and soda-ash removes the non-carbonate hardness
options.d: Lime reduces the non-carbonate hardness and soda-ash removes the carbonate hardness

solution: Sol(c). In lime-soda process Lime reduces the carbonate hardness and soda-ash removes the non-carbonate hardness

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The major disadvantage of lime soda process of water softening is that

options.a: It is unsuitable for turbid and acidic water
options.b: Huge amount of precipitate is formed which creates a disposal problem
options.c: The effluent cannot be reduced to zero hardness
options.d: It is unsuitable for softening the water of excessive hardness

solution: Sol(b). The major disadvantage of lime soda process of water softening is that huge amount of precipitate is formed which creates a disposal problem.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following compounds is widely used for algae control?
solution: Sol(b). Copper sulphate is widely used for algae control.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Activated carbon is used for
solution:
Sol(c). Application of activated carbon
- Gas purification
- Water purification
- Waste water treatment

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: As compared to cast iron pipes, steel pipes are
solution:
Sol(b). Steel pipes are stronger as compared to cast iron pipes
- Cast iron pipes was historically used as a pressure pipe for transmission of water, gas and sewage, and as a water drainage pipe.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The suitable layout of a distribution system for irregularly growing town is
solution: Sol(a).

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The layout of distribution system in which water flows towards the outer periphery is
solution: Sol(c). In Radial system water flows towards the outer periphery.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The suitable layout of distribution system for a city with roads of rectangular pattern is
solution: Sol(a). The suitable layout of distribution system for a city with roads of rectangular pattern is Grid iron system.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The commonly used material for water supply pipes, which has the properties of being strong, not easily corroded and long life but is heavy and brittle is
solution: Sol(b). Cast iron used for water supply pipes, which has the properties of being strong, not easily corroded and long life but is heavy and brittle.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Hardy cross method of analysis of distribution system
(i) Involves successive trials
(ii) Takes economic aspects into account
(iii) Is time consuming

The correct answer is
solution:
Sol(c). Hardy cross method of analysis of distribution system involves
- Successive trials
- Is time consuming

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The method of analysis of distribution system in which the domestic supply is neglected and fire demand is considered is
solution:
Sol(a). Circle method of analysis of distribution system in which the domestic supply is neglected and fire demand is considered.
- Ring system or circular system in which the main pipe line is provided around the city or area i.e., peripherally. From this main line, the branch lines are projected perpendicularly and they are also connected with each other.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following methods of analysis of water distribution, system is most suitable for long and narrow pipe system?
solution: Sol(b). Equivalent pipe methods of analysis of water distribution system is most suitable for long and narrow pipe system.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The type of valve which is provided to control the flow of water in the distribution system at street comers and where the pipe lines intersect is
solution: Sol(b). Sluice valve is provided to control the flow of water in the distribution system at street comers and where the pipe lines intersect.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The type of valve which allows water to flow in one direction but prevents its flow in the reverse direction is
solution: Sol(a). Reflux valve is commonly known as Check valve and Non-Return Valve (NRV). Water tends to flow from higher pressure to lower pressure.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Scour valves are provided

options.a: At street comers to control the flow of water
options.b: At every depression and dead ends to drain out the waste water that may collect there
options.c: At the foot of rising main along the slope to prevent back running of water
options.d: At every summit of rising mains

solution: Sol(b). Scour valves are located at low points or between valved sections of the pipeline. Their function is to allow periodic flushing of the lines to remove sediment and to allow the line to be drained for maintenance and repair work.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: A sewer that receives the discharge of a number of house sewers is called
solution: Sol(b). The building, or house, sewer is the smallest pipe in a wastewater collection system. Lateral sewer is the Piping that receives discharge from house sewers.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: A pipe conveying sewage from plumbing system of a single building to common sewer or point of immediate disposal is called
solution: Sol(a). House sewer is a prolongation of a house drain extending from a few feet outside a foundation to a connection with a public sewer in the street or alley also called building sewer

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: For a country like India, where rainfall is mainly confined to one season, the suitable sewerage system will be
solution: Sol(a). Separate system is suitable sewerage system for a country like India, where rainfall is mainly confined to one season.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Average rate of water consumption per head per day as per Indian Standard is
solution: Sol(b). BIS Standard IS:1172-1993 explains water consumption practices and norms for various uses. Also as reported in some other places, it is considered that the specific water consumption in India is around 145 lit per person per day.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Sewerage system is usually designed for
solution: Sol(b). Sewerage system is usually designed for 25 years.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following sewers is preferred for combined system of sewage?
solution:
Sol(b). Egg shaped sewer is preferred for combined system of sewage.
- The main advantage of an egg-shaped sewer is that for small flows the depth is greater and the velocity is somewhat higher than in a circular sewer of equivalent capacity. Thus, egg-shaped sewers have better self-cleansing property at small flows as compared to circular sewers.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The suitable system of sanitation for area of distributed rainfall throughout the year with less intensity is
solution: Sol(b). Combined system is suitable system of sanitation for area of distributed rainfall throughout the year with less intensity.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The water carriage system of collection of waste product

options.a: Is cheaper in initial cost than dry conservancy system

solution:
Sol(b). The water carriage system of collection of waste product requires treatment before disposal.
- In this system water is used as a medium to carry wastes to the point of final disposal. The quantity of water is so high (99.9%) that wastes becomes liquid which is carried by the sewers.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If the time of concentration is 9 minutes, then the intensity of rainfall according to British Ministry of Health formula Will be
solution:
Sol(d). Since time of concentration is between 5 and 20 minutes.
Hence intensity of rainfall = $\frac{760}{t+10} = \frac{760}{9+10} = 40$ mm/hr.
Note - Some literature suggests intensity of rainfall for time of concentration between 5 to 20 mins
$\frac{750}{t+10}$

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The time of concentration is defined as

options.a: The time taken by rainfall water to run from most distant point of water shed to the inlet of sewer
options.b: The time required for flow of water in sewer to the point under consideration

solution:
Sol(c). The time of concentration is defined as the period after which the entire catchment area will start contributing to the runoff.
- Time of concentration ($T_c$) is the time required for runoff to travel from the hydraulically most distant point in the watershed to the outlet.
- The time of concentration is equal to sum of inlet time and time of travel In sewers,
- Inlet time = The time taken by rainfall water to run from most distant point of water shed to the inlet of sewer.
- Time of travel = The time required for flow of water in sewer to the point under consideration.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The specific gravity of sewage is
solution: Sol(d). The specific gravity of sewage is slightly greater than 1.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The self-cleansing velocity for all sewers in India is usually
solution: Sol(b). The self-cleansing velocity for all sewers in India is usually 1.0 m/sec to 1.2 m/sec

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The slope of sewer shall be

options.a: Given in the direction of natural slope of ground
options.b: Given in the direction opposite to natural slope of ground

solution: Sol(a). The slope of sewer shall be given in the direction of natural slope of ground.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The design discharge for the separate sewer system shall be taken as
solution: Sol(d). The design discharge for the separate sewer system shall be taken as 6 x DWF.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The design discharge for the combined sewer system shall be taken as
solution:
Sol(c). The rain run-off i.e. drainage discharge, which is produced during monsoon season is generally very high, say 20 to 25 times that of the sewage discharge, called dry weather flow (DWF).
- The design discharge for the combined sewerage system shall be taken as = Rainfall + 2 DWF (Dry Weather Flow)

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The minimum and maximum diameters of sewers shall preferably be
solution:
Sol(b). The minimum diameter for plain and rolling terrain sewers generally adopted in the design may be 150 mm and for hilly area it is taken 100 mm.
- The maximum diameter of sewers is adopted in design as 300 cm.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The main disadvantage of cement concrete sewers

options.c: Difficulty in transportation due to heavy weight

solution: Sol(c). The main disadvantage of cement concrete sewers is difficulty in transportation due to heavy weight.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Most suitable section of sewer in separate sewage system is
solution:
Sol(b). For seperate system circular section is preferred.
- For Combined System egg Shaped, because lower portion of egg shaped sewer is used in low discharge and upper large section used in high discharge condition.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: An egg-shaped section of sewer

options.b: Provides self-cleansing velocity at low discharges
options.c: Is more stable than circular section

solution: Sol(b). An egg-shaped section of sewer provides self-cleansing velocity at low discharges.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The velocity of flow does not depend on
solution:
Sol(b). The velocity of flow depends on
- Grade of sewer
- Hydraulic mean depth of sewer
- Roughness of sewer

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The hydraulic mean depth (HMD) for an egg-shaped sewer flowing two-third full is

options.a: Equal to HMD when flowing full
options.b: Less than HMD when flowing full
options.c: Greater than HMD when flowing full

solution: Sol(c). The hydraulic mean depth (HMD) for an egg-shaped sewer flowing two-third full is greater than HMD when flowing full.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The effect of increasing diameter of sewer on the self-cleansing velocity is
solution:
Sol(b). Self-cleansing velocity
$V_s = \frac{1}{n} r^{\frac{1}{6}} \sqrt{k d' (G - 1)}$
for circular pipes running full, $r = \frac{D}{4}$
$V_s \propto \left(\frac{D}{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{6}}$
Assuming all parameters constant
Where n = manning's roughness coefficient
r = hydraulic mean radius
d' = grain particle size
G = specific gravity
- The effect of increasing the diameter of sewer on the self-cleansing velocity is to increase it.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The most commonly used sewer under culverts is
solution: Sol(a). The most commonly used sewer under culverts is circular brick sewer.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The type of sewer which is suitable for both combined and separate system is
solution: Sol(b). Egg shaped sewer is suitable for both combined and separate system.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The characteristics of fresh and septic sewage respectively are
solution:
Sol(b). Characteristics of fresh sewage is alkaline.
- Characteristics of septic sewage is acidic.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The pathogens can be killed by
solution: Sol(b). The process of killing pathogenic bacteria is known as disinfection and chlorination is one of the methods of disinfection.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following retards the self-purification of stream?
solution: Sol(d). Higher temperature, sunlight and satisfying oxygen demand are helps in self-purification process.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Sewage treatment units are normally designed for
solution: Sol(b). Sewage treatment units are normally designed for 15 - 20 years.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Settling velocity does not depend on
solution:
Sol(c). Settling velocity is given by where
$v = \frac{(\rho_s - \rho_f) g D^2}{18 \mu}$
$v$ = terminal velocity
$\rho_s$ = density of solids
$\rho_f$ = density of fluid
g = acceleration due to gravity
D = diameter of the particle
$\mu$ = viscosity of fluid
- From above formula we can say that settling velocity does not depend on depth of tank.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Standard BOD is measured at
solution: Sol(c). Standard BOD is measured at 20°C - 5 day.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The correct relation between theoretical' oxygen demand (ThOD), Biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) and Chemical oxygen demand (COD) is given by
solution:
Sol(b). The correct relation between ThOD, BOD and COD is ThOD > COD > BOD
- Theoretical oxygen demand (ThOD) is the calculated amount of oxygen required to oxidize a compound to its final oxidation products.
- COD or Chemical Oxygen Demand is the total measurement of all chemicals (organics & in-organics) in the water / waste water.
- BOD is a measure of, the amount of oxygen that require for the bacteria to degrade the organic components present in water / waste water.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Select the correct statement.

options.a: 5 day BOD is the ultimate BOD.
options.b: 5 day BOD is greater than 4 day BOD keeping other conditions same.
options.c: 5 day BOD is less than 4 day BOD keeping other conditions same.
options.d: BOD does not depend on time.

solution: Sol(c). In 5-day, BOD, micro-organisms have more time to consume O2 and hence dissolved oxygen demanded will be less after 5days whereas in 4day BOD micro-organisms have less time to consume O2 to decompose organic matter and after 4day dissolved oxygen demanded will be more to decompose organic matter.so BOD 5 is less than BOD 4.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If Biochemical oxygen demand (BOD) of a town is 20000 kg/day and BOD per capita per day is 0.05 kg, then population equivalent of town is
solution: Sol(d). Population equivalent = $\frac{\text{BOD of town } (\frac{\text{kg}}{\text{d}})}{\text{BOD of per capita } (\frac{\text{kg}}{\text{d} \cdot \text{person}})} = \frac{20000}{0.05} = 400000$

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The rate of BOD exerted at any time is
solution:
Sol(b). The rate of BOD exerted at any time is directly proportional to BOD remaining.
BOD is given by -
$L_t = L_0 e^{-k_1 t}$
where, $L_0$: Ultimate BOD, mg/L
$L_t$: BOD remaining at any time, mg/L
$k_1$: 1st order reaction rate constant, 1/d
t: time, d
But $L_0 = L_t + Y$
$\therefore Y = L_0(1 - e^{-k_1 t})$
where, Y: BOD Exerted at that time

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The ratio of 5 day BOD to ultimate BOD is about
solution: Sol(b). The ratio of 5 day BOD to ultimate BOD is about 2/3.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The minimum dissolved oxygen which should always be present in water in order to save the aquatic life is
solution: The minimum dissolved oxygen which should always be present in water in order to save the aquatic life is 4 ppm.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The relative stability of a sewage sample, whose dissolved oxygen is same as the total oxygen required to satisfy BOD, is
solution: Relative stability of a sewage sample, whose dissolved oxygen is same as the total oxygen required to satisfy BOD, is 100.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Dissolved oxygen in streams is
solution: At noon, rate of photosynthesis is high and hence the amount of oxygen produced exceeds the amount required for respiration. Therefore, the dissolved oxygen in streams is maximum at noon.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Facultative bacteria are able to work in

options.c: Presence as well as in absence of oxygen

solution: Facultative bacteria are able to work in presence as well as in absence of oxygen.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The means of access for inspection and cleaning of sewer line is known as
solution: The main purpose of a manhole is: To perform inspection, cleaning, and removal of any obstruction present in the sewage line.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Sewerage system is designed for
solution: The conventional sewers shall be designed for a minimum sewage flow of 100 litres per capita per day or higher as the case may be.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Sewage treatment units are designed for
solution: Sewage treatment units are designed for Average flow only.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Laying of sewers is usually done with the help of
solution: Laying of sewers is usually done by reference line method and by sight rail and boning Rod method.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Corrosion in concrete sewers is caused by
solution: Corrosion in concrete sewers is mainly caused by Hydrogen sulphide gas which is formed due to anaerobic decomposition of sewage (septic conditions).

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If the sewage contains grease and fatty oils, these are removed in
solution: If the sewage contains grease and fatty oils, these are removed in Skimming tanks.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Generally, the detention period for grit chambers is kept as
solution: Generally, the detention period for grit chambers is kept as 1 minute.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Which of the following unit works in anaerobic conditions?
solution: The conditions in sludge digestion tank is kept anaerobic.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Septic tank is a
i) Settling tank
ii) Digestion tank
iii) Aeration tank

The correct answer is
solution: Septic tank usually made of concrete or fiberglass is a small sewage treatment which mainly collects the waste generated by the domestic household. Septic tank works like both settling and digestion tank.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The maximum efficiency of BOD removal is achieved in
solution: The maximum efficiency of the BOD removal is obtained in oxidation ditch.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The working conditions in imhoff tanks are

options.c: Aerobic in lower compartment and anaerobic in upper compartment
options.d: Anaerobic in lower compartment and aerobic in upper compartment

solution: The working conditions in imhoff tanks are anaerobic in lower compartment and aerobic in upper compartment.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In facultative stabilization pond, the sewage is treated by

options.c: Dual action of aerobic bacteria and anaerobic bacteria

solution: In facultative stabilization pond, the sewage is treated by dual action of aerobic bacteria and anaerobic bacteria.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The detention period for oxidation ponds is usually kept as
solution: The detention period for oxidation ponds is usually kept as 10 to 15 days.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Composting and lagooning are the methods of
solution: Composting and lagooning are the methods of sludge disposal.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The main disadvantage of oxidation pond is that

options.a: Large area is required for construction
options.b: Maintenance and operation cost are high

solution: The main disadvantage of oxidation pond is that Large area is required for construction.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: For satisfactory working of a sludge digestion unit, the pH range of digested sludge should be maintained as
solution: For satisfactory working of a sludge digestion unit, the pH range of digested sludge should be maintained as 6.5 to 8.0.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Sludge volume index is defined as the ratio of

options.a: Percentage of sludge by volume to percentage of suspended solids by weight
options.b: Percentage of sludge by volume to percentage of total solids by weight
options.c: Percentage of suspended solids by weight to percentage of sludge by volume
options.d: Percentage of total solids by weight to percentage of sludge by volume

solution: Sludge volume index is defined as the ratio of percentage of sludge by volume to percentage of suspended solids by weight.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: For normal sludge, the value of sludge index for Indian conditions is
solution: For normal sludge, the value of sludge index for Indian conditions is 50 to 150.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: When there is no recirculation of sewage, then recirculation factor is
solution: Recirculation factor $F = \frac{1+R}{(1+0.1R)^2}$ where $R = \frac{Q_R}{Q_0} \rightarrow$ if $Q_R = 0$, then $F = 1$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: For the same solid content, if the quantity of sludge with moisture content of 98% is X, then the quantity of sludge with moisture content of 96% will be
solution: $V(100-p) = V_1(100-P_1)$ where $V$ = volume of sludge of moisture content $p$ (in %), $V_1$ = Volume of sludge at moisture content $p_1$ (in %). $X(100-98) = V_1(100-96) \Rightarrow V_1 = \frac{X}{2}$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: A pipe which is installed in the house drainage to preserve the water seal of traps is called
solution: A pipe which is installed in the house drainage to preserve the water seal of traps is called antisiphonage pipe.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In the two-pipe system of house plumbing, the pipes required are

options.a: One soil pipe, one waste pipe and one vent pipe
options.b: One soil pipe, two waste pipes and one vent pipe
options.c: One soil pipe, one waste pipe and two vent pipes
options.d: Two soil pipes, one waste pipe and one vent pipe

solution: Two pipe system - separate soil pipe and waste pipe or sullage pipe + ventilation pipe.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The pipe which is used to carry the discharge from sanitary fittings like bathrooms, kitchens etc. is called
solution: The pipe which is used to carry the discharge from sanitary fittings like bathrooms, kitchens etc. is called waste pipe.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The gas from sludge digestion tank is mainly composed of
solution: The gas from sludge digestion tank is mainly composed of methane.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Most of the bacteria in sewage are
solution: Bacteria from sewage are pathogenic, non-pathogenic, saprophytes autotrophic, heterotrophic, saprophytes etc. Saprophytic bacteria, which help to break down waste into inorganic and organic by-products. These bacteria are harmless and play a critical role in the treatment of sewage.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The process of lagooning is primarily a means of

options.a: Reducing the excessive flow in sewers
options.c: Increasing the capacity of storage reservoirs
options.d: Increasing flow of sewage through Imhoff tanks

solution: The process of lagooning is primarily a means of disposing of sludge.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The biochemical treatment of sewage effluents is essentially a process of
solution: The biochemical treatment of sewage effluents is essentially a process of Oxidation.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: To divert excessive flow from combined sewers, the most commonly used weir is a
solution: Leaping weir - The normal storm flow is diverted into the intercepting sewer and the excess flow leaps over the combined sewer to flow into the nearby stream.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Chlorine is used in the treatment of sewage to
solution: Chlorine is used as a disinfectant in sewage treatment plants. Sewage is treated to remove all the chemical and biological hazards, making it safe to release into water bodies. Chlorine also oxidises a large number of organic molecules such as lipids and proteins and also helps in grease separation.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: In the design of grit chambers

options.c: The detention period should be at least 30 minutes
options.d: The maximum velocity of flow is 30 cm per second

solution: In the design of grit chambers horizontal velocity of flow of 15 to 30 cm/sec is used at peak flows.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
Match List I with List II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists.

List I
A. Acidity
B. Alum
C. Cyclone
D. Skimming tank

List II
1. Particulate matter
2. Oils and greases
3. Flocculation
4. Carbon dioxide

Codes:
solution: Carbon dioxide causes acidity in water. Particulate matter is removed by cyclone precipitator. Oils and greases are removed in skimming tank. Alum is used as coagulant in flocculation process.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text:
If the diameter of the main pipe is taken less than economic diameter then
1. head loss will be high
2. cost of pipe will be less
3. cost of pumping will be less
Of these statements
solution: If the diameter of the main pipe is taken less than economic diameter then Head loss will be high, Cost of pipe will be less, Cost of pumping will be high.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Incrustation of the tubewell pipes may result in

options.a: discharge of highly corrosive acid water
options.d: excessive discharge of sand with water

solution: The deposition of alkali salts on the inside walls of the pipe is called incrustation. It reduces the effective diameter of the well pipe and hence reduces the discharge of the tubewell.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The maximum velocity occurs in an egg shaped sewer when the ratio of depth of flow to vertical diameter is
solution: The maximum velocity occurs in an egg shaped sewer when the ratio of depth of flow to vertical diameter is 0.81.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Shrouding is provided in
solution: Shrouding is the stones or aggregates provided around the slotted pipe at the bottom of the pipe.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If in a test conducted for determining relative stability, the period of incubation at $20^\circ\text{C}$ is t days, the relative stability in % is given by
solution: Relative stability when temperature is $20^\circ\text{C}$ is $S = 100(1-0.794)^{t_{20}}$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Ozone layer in the upper atmosphere is getting destroyed owing to its reaction with
solution: Ozone layer is formed in the stratosphere of the atmosphere by the action of UV rays on the oxygen molecules. However, the ozone depleting pollutants like chlorofluorocarbons reach the stratosphere and break when exposed to the UV rays.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The intensification of green house effect is attributed to the increased level of
solution: Greenhouse effect is a naturally occurring phenomenon, it is possible that the effect could be intensified by the emission of greenhouse gases into the atmosphere as the result of human activity. From the beginning of the Industrial Revolution through the end of the 20th century, the amount of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere increased by roughly 30 percent and the amount of methane more than doubled.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Electrostatic precipitators are used as pollution control device for the separation of
solution: Electrostatic precipitators are used for air pollution control, particularly for removing particles from waste gases at industrial facilities and power-generating stations.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Select the secondary air pollutants among the following:
solution: Different types of secondary pollutants include: Ozone ($\text{O}_3$), Sulfuric acid and nitric acid (component of acid rain), Particulate matter, Nitrogen dioxide ($\text{NO}_2$), Peroxyacyl nitrates (PANs) and more.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The primary pollutant caused by incomplete combustion of organic matter is
solution: The primary pollutant caused by incomplete combustion of organic matter is carbon monoxide.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Select the primary air pollutants among the following :
solution: Sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are the primary pollutants, ozone is secondary pollutant.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The specified standard under US Ambient Air Quality standards for $\text{SO}_2$, $\text{NO}_2$ and $\text{CO}$ respectively are
solution: US National Ambient air quality standards are provided in the table.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The spray tower can be used to control
solution: Spray towers are inexpensive control devices primarily used for gas conditioning (cooling or humidifying) or for first-stage particle or gas removal.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The specified standard for $\text{SO}_2$ under US Ambient Air Quality standards is $80\ \mu\text{g/m}^3$. This is approximately equivalent to
solution: $1\text{ ppm} = \frac{MP}{RT}\ \mu\text{g/m}^3$. For $\text{SO}_2$, $M = 64\text{ g/mol}$. $80\ \mu\text{g/m}^3 \approx 0.03\text{ ppm}$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The depletion of ozone in the outer atmosphere may result in increased
solution: The depletion of ozone in the outer atmosphere may result in increased skin cancer cases.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The rain is generally termed as acidic, if ph value of rain water falls below
solution: The rain is generally termed as acidic, if ph value of rain water falls below 5.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The acid rains are caused by the following pollutants:
solution: The acid rains are caused by the $\text{SO}_2$ and $\text{NO}_x$ pollutants.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: To express sound levels in decibels, sound pressure levels are usually adopted on a reference scale of
solution: Out of the three terms given above, sound pressure level on reference scale $20\ \mu\text{Pa}$ is usually adopted to express sound levels in decibels.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: As per National Ambient Air Quality standards, maximum permissible concentration of $\text{NO}_2$ (24 hr. average) in ambient air in residential area is:
solution: As per national Ambient air quality standards maximum permissible concentration of $\text{NO}_2$ (24 hr. average) in ambient air in residential area is $80\ \mu\text{g/m}^3$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: The height of chimney required for effective disposal of $27\text{ kg/hr}$ of $\text{SO}_2$ emission is
solution: As per CPCB, the stack height $H = 14 \times Q^{\frac{1}{3}}\text{ m}$ where Q is $\text{SO}_2$ emissions in kg/hr. So, $H = 14 \times 27^{\frac{1}{3}} = 42\text{ m}$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: If the actual height of a stack is 150 m and plume height is 8 m, then the effective height of the stack is
solution: Effective Stack Height $H = h + \Delta H$ Where: H = Effective stack height (m), h = height of physical stack (m), $\Delta H$ = plume rise (m). So, $H = 150 + 8 = 158\text{ m}$.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: When the Adiabatic Lapse Rate (ALR) is more than Environmental Lapse Rate (ELR), then the ELR can be called as
solution: When ELR < ALR than it is known as sub adiabatic lapse rate and the plume which is formed in this condition is coning plume.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: When Environmental Lapse Rate (ELR) is more than Adiabatic Lapse Rate (ALR), then the environment is said to be
solution: If ELR > ALR than it is known as super adiabatic lapse rate and the environment is said to be highly unstable.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Temporary hardness in water is caused by the presence of
solution: Temporary hardness in water is caused by the presence of bicarbonate of Ca and Mg.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Blue baby disease (methemoglobinemia) in children is caused by the presence of excess
solution: Blue baby disease (methemoglobinemia) in children is caused by the presence of excess nitrates.

subject: Environmental Engineering
question_text: Two samples of water A and B have pH values of 4.4 and 6.4 respectively. How many times more acidic sample A is than sample B?
solution:
For sample A:
$\text{pH} = -\log[\text{H}^+] \Rightarrow 4.4 = -\log[\text{H}^+] \Rightarrow [\text{H}^+] = 10^{-4.4}\text{ mol/lit}$

For sample B:
$\text{pH} = -\log[\text{H}^+] \Rightarrow 6.4 = -\log[\text{H}^+] \Rightarrow [\text{H}^+] = 10^{-6.4}\text{ mol/lit}$

$\frac{[\text{H}^+]\text{ of A}}{[\text{H}^+]\text{ of B}} = \frac{10^{-4.4}}{10^{-6.4}} = 100$

So, sample A is 100 times more acidic than sample B.
